title: Invalid URL error in HTTP Request node
symptom: The HTTP Request node throws an 'Invalid URL' error when executing a workflow. This occurs when the URL field is not correctly formatted.
applies_when: URL field is not properly configured
not_this_if: URL is correctly formatted and surrounded by curly braces
root_cause: The error occurs because the URL field in the HTTP Request node is not set up correctly. Specifically, if the URL is not surrounded by curly braces and is not set to expression type, n8n cannot interpret it as a valid URL, leading to the 'Invalid URL' error.
fix_steps: Open the HTTP Request node settings
Ensure the URL field is set to '{{ $json.sanitizedUrl }}'
Change the field type to expression
Save the workflow and try executing it again
agent_instruction: Set the URL field in the HTTP Request node to '{{ $json.sanitizedUrl }}' and change it to expression type.
